spotlight: Munich elected its first-ever Green mayor, ending 42 uninterrupted years of SPD rule in the Bavarian capital.

stories.title: Government weighs cheaper electricity but rules out diesel tax cut

body.text: Deputy prime minister and industry minister Karel Havlíček (ANO) said he does not intend to lower the excise tax on diesel, but is considering relief in the regulated component of the electricity price as a way to ease costs for households and firms.

In a separate interview Havlíček elaborated on the broader energy agenda, including how to secure sufficient gas to fill storage facilities ahead of next winter, the planned re-nationalisation of ČEZ and an American LNG contract. He framed the search for a mechanism to guarantee adequate gas supplies for the coming heating season as a priority amid uncertainty over deliveries.

shortBody.text: Deputy prime minister and industry minister Karel Havlíček (ANO) said he does not intend to lower the excise tax on diesel, but is considering relief in the regulated component of the electricity price as a way to ease costs for households and firms.

In a separate interview Havlíček elaborated on the broader energy agenda, including how to secure sufficient gas to fill storage facilities ahead of next winter, the planned re-nationalisation of ČEZ and an American LNG contract. He framed the search for a mechanism to guarantee adequate gas supplies for the coming heating season as a priority amid uncertainty over deliveries.

stories.title: Schillerová unveils new retail Republic Bonds issue

body.text: Finance minister Alena Schillerová presented a new issue of the state's Republic Bonds (Dluhopisy republiky), allowing households once again to lend money directly to the state. The retail-focused instrument lets individual investors place their savings with the government.
The offering revives a retail savings channel that gives the Treasury an additional way to finance public borrowing outside wholesale debt markets.

shortBody.text: Finance minister Alena Schillerová presented a new issue of the state's Republic Bonds (Dluhopisy republiky), allowing households once again to lend money directly to the state. The retail-focused instrument lets individual investors place their savings with the government.
The offering revives a retail savings channel that gives the Treasury an additional way to finance public borrowing outside wholesale debt markets.

stories.title: Transport contracts worth up to CZK 19bn start being signed

body.text: Following the president's signature of the budget law, the Road and Motorway Directorate (ŘSD) and the Railway Administration have begun an unusually large wave of contract signings for transport projects worth up to CZK 19bn.
The signings cover both road and rail infrastructure, unblocking projects that had awaited the budget's adoption.

shortBody.text: Following the president's signature of the budget law, the Road and Motorway Directorate (ŘSD) and the Railway Administration have begun an unusually large wave of contract signings for transport projects worth up to CZK 19bn.
The signings cover both road and rail infrastructure, unblocking projects that had awaited the budget's adoption.

stories.title: Czech municipalities post deficit and ramp up investment

body.text: For the first time in 15 years, Czech municipalities excluding Prague reported a budget deficit last year, ending a long period of accumulating surpluses.

Investment spending rose by a quarter, with the approaching municipal elections cited as a driver of the increased outlays. The shift marks a departure from years of conservative hoarding of reserves at the local level.

shortBody.text: For the first time in 15 years, Czech municipalities excluding Prague reported a budget deficit last year, ending a long period of accumulating surpluses.

Investment spending rose by a quarter, with the approaching municipal elections cited as a driver of the increased outlays. The shift marks a departure from years of conservative hoarding of reserves at the local level.

stories.title: Audit finds questionable subsidies for road repairs

body.text: The Supreme Audit Office (NKÚ) found that more than half of the subsidies provided by the Ministry for Regional Development for repairs of municipal roads went to insignificant local communications.
The finding raises questions about how the grant scheme targets its funds.

shortBody.text: The Supreme Audit Office (NKÚ) found that more than half of the subsidies provided by the Ministry for Regional Development for repairs of municipal roads went to insignificant local communications.
The finding raises questions about how the grant scheme targets its funds.

stories.title: Italian U-Power acquires Czech Cerva Group

body.text: Italian workwear maker U-Power completed the acquisition of Czech company Cerva Group, creating a combined business with turnover exceeding CZK 12bn.
The deal consolidates two producers in the protective and work clothing sector under one ownership.

shortBody.text: Italian workwear maker U-Power completed the acquisition of Czech company Cerva Group, creating a combined business with turnover exceeding CZK 12bn.
The deal consolidates two producers in the protective and work clothing sector under one ownership.

stories.title: Indebted Solek presents new reorganisation plan

body.text: Solar group Solek presented an updated reorganisation plan offering creditors lower repayment after a new valuation set its assets at CZK 83m.
The revised proposal reflects the reduced asset base of the indebted company.

shortBody.text: Solar group Solek presented an updated reorganisation plan offering creditors lower repayment after a new valuation set its assets at CZK 83m.
The revised proposal reflects the reduced asset base of the indebted company.

stories.title: Czech investors close another billion-crown deal in Poland

body.text: Czech investors sealed another billion-crown transaction in Poland, acquiring an established shopping centre.
The purchase adds to a string of Czech real estate acquisitions in the Polish market.

shortBody.text: Czech investors sealed another billion-crown transaction in Poland, acquiring an established shopping centre.
The purchase adds to a string of Czech real estate acquisitions in the Polish market.

stories.title: Gulf war pushes European chemicals industry to breaking point

body.text: Europe's chemicals industry is being squeezed by a combination of high energy prices, Chinese competition and the fallout from the war in the Persian Gulf, pushing the sector to a breaking point.
The convergence of these pressures is straining producers' ability to remain competitive on global markets.

shortBody.text: Europe's chemicals industry is being squeezed by a combination of high energy prices, Chinese competition and the fallout from the war in the Persian Gulf, pushing the sector to a breaking point.
The convergence of these pressures is straining producers' ability to remain competitive on global markets.

stories.title: VW, BioNTech among German firms facing layoff wave

body.text: Volkswagen, BioNTech and other German companies have announced a wave of job cuts, with analysts warning it may only be the beginning.
The announcements span the automotive and biotech sectors, fuelling concern about the trajectory of German industrial employment.

shortBody.text: Volkswagen, BioNTech and other German companies have announced a wave of job cuts, with analysts warning it may only be the beginning.
The announcements span the automotive and biotech sectors, fuelling concern about the trajectory of German industrial employment.

stories.title: IEA warns of further oil price spikes as inventories plunge

body.text: The International Energy Agency warned of further price spikes for oil as global inventories drop at a record pace.
The rapid drawdown of stocks leaves the market exposed to renewed upward pressure on crude prices.

shortBody.text: The International Energy Agency warned of further price spikes for oil as global inventories drop at a record pace.
The rapid drawdown of stocks leaves the market exposed to renewed upward pressure on crude prices.

stories.title: Germany fears first energy shortages in summer

body.text: Germany is bracing for the possibility of its first energy rationing this summer, the result of unstable gas supplies following the war in the Persian Gulf.
The prospect of bottlenecks reflects how the Gulf conflict has reverberated through Europe's gas supply chain.

shortBody.text: Germany is bracing for the possibility of its first energy rationing this summer, the result of unstable gas supplies following the war in the Persian Gulf.
The prospect of bottlenecks reflects how the Gulf conflict has reverberated through Europe's gas supply chain.

stories.title: UN warns Middle East war threatens global food supply

body.text: The conflict in the Persian Gulf has disrupted supplies of fertilisers and energy, prompting the UN to warn that millions of people could go without food.
Russia and the United States are benefiting from the resulting rise in prices on food markets, according to the report.

shortBody.text: The conflict in the Persian Gulf has disrupted supplies of fertilisers and energy, prompting the UN to warn that millions of people could go without food.
Russia and the United States are benefiting from the resulting rise in prices on food markets, according to the report.

stories.title: Turkey raises inflation target as war adds to strain

body.text: Turkey's central bank lifted its inflation target as the economy continues to be hit by the impact of the war in the Persian Gulf.
The upward revision acknowledges the persistent price pressures weighing on the Turkish economy.

shortBody.text: Turkey's central bank lifted its inflation target as the economy continues to be hit by the impact of the war in the Persian Gulf.
The upward revision acknowledges the persistent price pressures weighing on the Turkish economy.

stories.title: Klarna breaks even for first time since listing

body.text: Swedish fintech Klarna reached break-even results for the first time since its blockbuster initial public offering.
The milestone marks a turn in the buy-now-pay-later group's profitability after its record-scale listing.

shortBody.text: Swedish fintech Klarna reached break-even results for the first time since its blockbuster initial public offering.
The milestone marks a turn in the buy-now-pay-later group's profitability after its record-scale listing.

stories.title: European AI stocks soar as US tech frenzy spreads

body.text: Investors are seeking out Europe's few AI plays, including STMicroelectronics, Aixtron and Nokia, whose share prices have climbed sharply this year as the US tech frenzy goes global.
The rally reflects the limited number of listed European companies offering exposure to the artificial intelligence theme.

shortBody.text: Investors are seeking out Europe's few AI plays, including STMicroelectronics, Aixtron and Nokia, whose share prices have climbed sharply this year as the US tech frenzy goes global.
The rally reflects the limited number of listed European companies offering exposure to the artificial intelligence theme.

stories.title: Honda posts first-ever annual loss after EV pullback

body.text: Honda reported the first annual loss in its history after pulling back its investments in electric vehicles.
The result follows the carmaker's retreat from electromobility spending.

shortBody.text: Honda reported the first annual loss in its history after pulling back its investments in electric vehicles.
The result follows the carmaker's retreat from electromobility spending.

stories.title: SpaceX IPO to lock in Musk control with Mars-linked pay

body.text: SpaceX is heading toward an IPO valued at $75bn that would cement Elon Musk's extraordinarily strong control of the company, paired with a billion-dollar pay package tied to establishing a colony on Mars.
The structure ensures Musk retains decisive influence over the rocket maker after it goes public.

shortBody.text: SpaceX is heading toward an IPO valued at $75bn that would cement Elon Musk's extraordinarily strong control of the company, paired with a billion-dollar pay package tied to establishing a colony on Mars.
The structure ensures Musk retains decisive influence over the rocket maker after it goes public.

stories.title: OpenAI's $500bn Stargate scheme shifts shape

body.text: OpenAI is restructuring its $500bn Stargate megaproject for AI infrastructure, adopting what is described as a shape-shifting approach.
The evolving design reflects the scale and complexity of the planned computing buildout.

shortBody.text: OpenAI is restructuring its $500bn Stargate megaproject for AI infrastructure, adopting what is described as a shape-shifting approach.
The evolving design reflects the scale and complexity of the planned computing buildout.

stories.title: McKinsey to cut partner cash in post-AI pay revamp

body.text: McKinsey is reforming partner pay, shifting a larger share to equity rather than cash, in response to AI-driven changes in the consulting industry.
The overhaul reflects how artificial intelligence is reshaping the economics of professional services.

shortBody.text: McKinsey is reforming partner pay, shifting a larger share to equity rather than cash, in response to AI-driven changes in the consulting industry.
The overhaul reflects how artificial intelligence is reshaping the economics of professional services.

stories.title: AI boom drives hunt for 'flying IT staff'

body.text: Forward deployed engineers — developers who travel to clients to implement AI — have become the most frequently opened position in Silicon Valley, a trend gradually spreading to the Czech market.
The role reflects rising corporate demand for hands-on AI deployment expertise amid the technology boom.

shortBody.text: Forward deployed engineers — developers who travel to clients to implement AI — have become the most frequently opened position in Silicon Valley, a trend gradually spreading to the Czech market.
The role reflects rising corporate demand for hands-on AI deployment expertise amid the technology boom.

stories.title: Xi warns Trump that Taiwan question defines US-China ties

body.text: At the opening of the Beijing summit, Xi Jinping warned Donald Trump that the Taiwan question is the critical point of US relations with China, with Washington-Beijing ties hinging on its handling.
Trump's stated intention to discuss American arms sales to Taiwan with Xi has rattled Asian allies, who fear it could become a bargaining chip in broader negotiations.

shortBody.text: At the opening of the Beijing summit, Xi Jinping warned Donald Trump that the Taiwan question is the critical point of US relations with China, with Washington-Beijing ties hinging on its handling.
Trump's stated intention to discuss American arms sales to Taiwan with Xi has rattled Asian allies, who fear it could become a bargaining chip in broader negotiations.

stories.title: Putin and Zelenskyy cool on US-led peace talks

body.text: Neither Russia nor Ukraine believes in a revival of US-brokered peace negotiations even after the end of the Middle East war, with both Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy cool on the prospect.

Analysts warn that Russia may launch a renewed spring offensive once the weather turns. Ukraine, meanwhile, is sending hundreds of drones deep into Russian targets daily and has struck the Primorsk oil terminal.

shortBody.text: Neither Russia nor Ukraine believes in a revival of US-brokered peace negotiations even after the end of the Middle East war, with both Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy cool on the prospect.

Analysts warn that Russia may launch a renewed spring offensive once the weather turns. Ukraine, meanwhile, is sending hundreds of drones deep into Russian targets daily and has struck the Primorsk oil terminal.

stories.title: Ukrainian arms maker aims to cut Kyiv's reliance on US

body.text: Ukrainian arms manufacturer Fire Point is launching its own satellites in a bid to reduce Kyiv's dependence on American technology.
The move is part of Ukraine's effort to build indigenous defence capabilities less reliant on US support.

shortBody.text: Ukrainian arms manufacturer Fire Point is launching its own satellites in a bid to reduce Kyiv's dependence on American technology.
The move is part of Ukraine's effort to build indigenous defence capabilities less reliant on US support.

stories.title: US abruptly cancels deployment of 4,000 troops to Poland

body.text: The Pentagon cancelled a planned deployment of 4,000 American troops to Poland, a decision that prompted unease across Europe.
The abrupt reversal raised questions about the US commitment to bolstering NATO's eastern flank.

shortBody.text: The Pentagon cancelled a planned deployment of 4,000 American troops to Poland, a decision that prompted unease across Europe.
The abrupt reversal raised questions about the US commitment to bolstering NATO's eastern flank.

stories.title: NATO foreign ministers meet in Sweden, eyes on Rubio
stories.author: Matthias Wyssuwa

body.text: NATO foreign ministers are meeting in Sweden, with all eyes on US Secretary of State Marco Rubio over Washington's position within the alliance.
The gathering takes place amid heightened uncertainty about American commitments to NATO.

shortBody.text: NATO foreign ministers are meeting in Sweden, with all eyes on US Secretary of State Marco Rubio over Washington's position within the alliance.
The gathering takes place amid heightened uncertainty about American commitments to NATO.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia floats non-aggression pact with Iran

body.text: Saudi Arabia is proposing a non-aggression pact with Iran in response to the US-Israeli military operation against Tehran.
US officials say Saudi Arabia and the UAE carried out secret attacks inside Iran.

At the same time, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain and Qatar want Washington to ensure that the US-Israeli operation ends with Iran militarily weakened, urging Trump to destroy Tehran's military capabilities now that the war has begun.

shortBody.text: Saudi Arabia is proposing a non-aggression pact with Iran in response to the US-Israeli military operation against Tehran.
US officials say Saudi Arabia and the UAE carried out secret attacks inside Iran.

At the same time, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain and Qatar want Washington to ensure that the US-Israeli operation ends with Iran militarily weakened, urging Trump to destroy Tehran's military capabilities now that the war has begun.

stories.title: Lebanon and Israel to meet in Washington as ceasefire nears end

body.text: Lebanon and Israel are preparing for a third round of direct talks in Washington over extending their ceasefire as the current truce nears its end.
The negotiations aim to prevent a renewed escalation along the border.

shortBody.text: Lebanon and Israel are preparing for a third round of direct talks in Washington over extending their ceasefire as the current truce nears its end.
The negotiations aim to prevent a renewed escalation along the border.

stories.title: Merz vows to oppose new EU debt despite German spending

body.text: German chancellor Friedrich Merz refuses to back further joint EU debt, despite his own government's German budget package.
His stance sets up a potential clash over common European borrowing even as Berlin expands domestic spending.

shortBody.text: German chancellor Friedrich Merz refuses to back further joint EU debt, despite his own government's German budget package.
His stance sets up a potential clash over common European borrowing even as Berlin expands domestic spending.

stories.title: Higher education reform to curb academic senates

body.text: The education minister presented a reform of higher education that would weaken academic senates while strengthening the powers of rectors and creating a new university council.
The proposed governance changes would shift the balance of power within Czech universities.

shortBody.text: The education minister presented a reform of higher education that would weaken academic senates while strengthening the powers of rectors and creating a new university council.
The proposed governance changes would shift the balance of power within Czech universities.

stories.title: Plaga admits radical cut of secondary school fields failed
stories.author: Markéta Hronová

body.text: New education minister Plaga (ANO) acknowledged that the planned radical cut in the number of secondary school study fields did not succeed, and the reform is being postponed by a year.
Plaga faces the question of whether he can modernise secondary education or whether the effort will again stall at promises.

shortBody.text: New education minister Plaga (ANO) acknowledged that the planned radical cut in the number of secondary school study fields did not succeed, and the reform is being postponed by a year.
Plaga faces the question of whether he can modernise secondary education or whether the effort will again stall at promises.

stories.title: Further turmoil at Juchelka's ministry as deputy resigns

body.text: Fresh turmoil hit minister Juchelka's department as a deputy minister resigned and a second adviser lost his post.
The departures add to instability in the ministry's leadership.

shortBody.text: Fresh turmoil hit minister Juchelka's department as a deputy minister resigned and a second adviser lost his post.
The departures add to instability in the ministry's leadership.

stories.title: NSA chief plans CZK 8.1bn sports funding overhaul
stories.author: Jan Prokeš

body.text: New National Sports Agency chairman Karel Kovář outlined plans to distribute CZK 8.1bn into sport, with allocations to depend not only on medals but also on the number of members in clubs.
He also described plans to draw on European subsidies for sports financing.

shortBody.text: New National Sports Agency chairman Karel Kovář outlined plans to distribute CZK 8.1bn into sport, with allocations to depend not only on medals but also on the number of members in clubs.
He also described plans to draw on European subsidies for sports financing.

stories.title: Court rejects 25-year Teslamp claim against Česká spořitelna
stories.author: Marek Pokorný

body.text: A court dismissed the 25-year-old lawsuit brought by the Teslamp insolvency administrator against Česká spořitelna, who had sought CZK 4bn.
Instead, the administrator must now pay more than CZK 100m to cover the bank's legal costs.

shortBody.text: A court dismissed the 25-year-old lawsuit brought by the Teslamp insolvency administrator against Česká spořitelna, who had sought CZK 4bn.
Instead, the administrator must now pay more than CZK 100m to cover the bank's legal costs.

stories.title: Munich elects first Green mayor, ending 42 years of SPD rule
stories.author: Václav Lavička

body.text: Thirty-five-year-old Dominik Krause of the Greens defeated Social Democrat Reiter to become mayor of Munich, ending 42 years of uninterrupted SPD government in the city.
It is the first time a Green politician has led the Bavarian capital.

shortBody.text: Thirty-five-year-old Dominik Krause of the Greens defeated Social Democrat Reiter to become mayor of Munich, ending 42 years of uninterrupted SPD government in the city.
It is the first time a Green politician has led the Bavarian capital.

stories.title: Erdoğan engineers breakup of Turkish opposition

body.text: Erdoğan is organising the disintegration of Turkey's opposition CHP, with a court stripping party leader Özel of his mandate in favour of former chief Kılıçdaroğlu.
The ruling reshapes the leadership of Turkey's main opposition force.

shortBody.text: Erdoğan is organising the disintegration of Turkey's opposition CHP, with a court stripping party leader Özel of his mandate in favour of former chief Kılıçdaroğlu.
The ruling reshapes the leadership of Turkey's main opposition force.

stories.title: Volker Beck reportedly targeted in Iran-linked plot
stories.author: Friederike Haupt

body.text: German politician Volker Beck was reportedly the target of an assassination plot apparently backed by Iran, and he criticises the slowness of the government's response.
Beck's case highlights concerns over Iranian threats on German soil.

shortBody.text: German politician Volker Beck was reportedly the target of an assassination plot apparently backed by Iran, and he criticises the slowness of the government's response.
Beck's case highlights concerns over Iranian threats on German soil.

stories.title: Posselt defends Niebler against EU funds accusation

body.text: Former MEP Bernd Posselt is defending Angelika Niebler (CSU) against accusations of misusing EU funds.
The dispute concerns alleged irregularities in the handling of European Parliament resources.

shortBody.text: Former MEP Bernd Posselt is defending Angelika Niebler (CSU) against accusations of misusing EU funds.
The dispute concerns alleged irregularities in the handling of European Parliament resources.

stories.title: US steps up pressure on Cuba with Castro accusation
stories.author: Tjerk Brühwiller

body.text: The US is intensifying pressure on Cuba with accusations against Raúl Castro, following a pattern similar to that seen with Venezuela's Maduro before military intervention.
The escalation raises the question of how far Washington intends to take its campaign.

shortBody.text: The US is intensifying pressure on Cuba with accusations against Raúl Castro, following a pattern similar to that seen with Venezuela's Maduro before military intervention.
The escalation raises the question of how far Washington intends to take its campaign.

stories.title: Starmer faces growing leadership challenge after Streeting quits

body.text: British prime minister Keir Starmer faces increasing intra-party pressure after minister Wes Streeting resigned, with a contest to unseat him looking increasingly likely.
The resignation has emboldened internal challengers within the governing party.

shortBody.text: British prime minister Keir Starmer faces increasing intra-party pressure after minister Wes Streeting resigned, with a contest to unseat him looking increasingly likely.
The resignation has emboldened internal challengers within the governing party.

stories.title: Slovak cave authority chief stole Czech colleagues' backpacks

body.text: The new head of the Slovak cave authority stole the backpacks of Czech colleagues and left them exposed to freezing conditions, a case now shaking the institution.
The affair has drawn scrutiny to the leadership of the body.

shortBody.text: The new head of the Slovak cave authority stole the backpacks of Czech colleagues and left them exposed to freezing conditions, a case now shaking the institution.
The affair has drawn scrutiny to the leadership of the body.

stories.title: US Supreme Court allows abortion pill access by mail to continue

body.text: The US Supreme Court allowed the mailing of the abortion pill to continue, despite pressure from conservative states.
The ruling preserves access to medication abortion by mail nationwide.

shortBody.text: The US Supreme Court allowed the mailing of the abortion pill to continue, despite pressure from conservative states.
The ruling preserves access to medication abortion by mail nationwide.

stories.title: Why Warsh wants the Fed to keep quiet

body.text: The commentary argues that new Fed chair Kevin Warsh plans to narrow the central bank's communication, including scrapping the 'dot plots' forecast.
The piece examines Warsh's rationale for a more reticent Fed approach to guidance.

shortBody.text: The commentary argues that new Fed chair Kevin Warsh plans to narrow the central bank's communication, including scrapping the 'dot plots' forecast.
The piece examines Warsh's rationale for a more reticent Fed approach to guidance.

stories.title: How Britain found itself in a debt cage

body.text: This FT Big Read offers a deep analysis of how Britain ended up trapped in a structure of high public debt after decades of neglected reforms.
The piece traces the accumulation of fiscal pressures that constrain the country's options.

shortBody.text: This FT Big Read offers a deep analysis of how Britain ended up trapped in a structure of high public debt after decades of neglected reforms.
The piece traces the accumulation of fiscal pressures that constrain the country's options.

stories.title: Trump was flattering, Xi was resolute

body.text: This NYT analysis contrasts the different demeanours of Trump and Xi during the Beijing summit — one flattering, the other firm — arguing the difference spoke volumes.
The piece reads the body language and tone as a signal of the balance of power between the two leaders.

shortBody.text: This NYT analysis contrasts the different demeanours of Trump and Xi during the Beijing summit — one flattering, the other firm — arguing the difference spoke volumes.
The piece reads the body language and tone as a signal of the balance of power between the two leaders.

stories.title: Bank results once again did not disappoint
stories.author: Karel Nedvěd

body.text: According to Fio banka analyst Karel Nedvěd, the fourth-quarter 2025 results of Erste, Komerční banka and Moneta were solid, offering a measure of certainty in turbulent times.
He frames the banks' performance as a reassuring constant amid broader uncertainty.

shortBody.text: According to Fio banka analyst Karel Nedvěd, the fourth-quarter 2025 results of Erste, Komerční banka and Moneta were solid, offering a measure of certainty in turbulent times.
He frames the banks' performance as a reassuring constant amid broader uncertainty.

stories.title: Are we facing another inflation jump?
stories.author: Julie Hrstková

body.text: Julie Hrstková writes about the risks of a new inflation spike linked to rising oil prices after the war in the Persian Gulf.
Her commentary draws a parallel to past inflationary episodes.

shortBody.text: Julie Hrstková writes about the risks of a new inflation spike linked to rising oil prices after the war in the Persian Gulf.
Her commentary draws a parallel to past inflationary episodes.

stories.title: Czech economy grew three times faster than Slovakia's
stories.author: Anna Šuhajdová

body.text: Anna Šuhajdová compares economic growth in the Czech Republic (2.6%) and Slovakia (0.8%), arguing the divergence will deepen this year as well.
She sees the gap between the two economies widening further.

shortBody.text: Anna Šuhajdová compares economic growth in the Czech Republic (2.6%) and Slovakia (0.8%), arguing the divergence will deepen this year as well.
She sees the gap between the two economies widening further.

stories.title: No offices are being built in Prague
stories.author: Pavel Kadera

body.text: Pavel Kadera writes that Prague's office market is experiencing a downturn in new construction, driven by two trends: developers being drawn to residential building, with offices paying off only in city centres.
He attributes the slowdown to a shift in developer priorities.

shortBody.text: Pavel Kadera writes that Prague's office market is experiencing a downturn in new construction, driven by two trends: developers being drawn to residential building, with offices paying off only in city centres.
He attributes the slowdown to a shift in developer priorities.

stories.title: Investment bubbles are a normal feature of markets
stories.author: Michal Stupavský

body.text: Conseq investment strategist Michal Stupavský discusses theories of investment bubbles according to Grantham, Shiller, Soros and Buffett, and the return of valuations to their average.
He frames bubbles as a recurring characteristic of financial markets.

shortBody.text: Conseq investment strategist Michal Stupavský discusses theories of investment bubbles according to Grantham, Shiller, Soros and Buffett, and the return of valuations to their average.

stories.title: Are fears about corporate debt justified?
stories.author: Jiří Król

body.text: Jiří Król writes about the private credit market, arguing that the debate over portfolio valuation and the impact of AI on the software sector is exaggerated.
He plays down concerns over the quality of corporate debt.

shortBody.text: Jiří Król writes about the private credit market, arguing that the debate over portfolio valuation and the impact of AI on the software sector is exaggerated.
He plays down concerns over the quality of corporate debt.

stories.title: How dangerous is the flood of private credit?

body.text: This FAZ analysis examines the private credit market, pointing to rising fund withdrawals, valuation questions and concerns about the impact of AI on the software sector.
The piece weighs the systemic risks posed by the growth of private lending.

shortBody.text: This FAZ analysis examines the private credit market, pointing to rising fund withdrawals, valuation questions and concerns about the impact of AI on the software sector.
The piece weighs the systemic risks posed by the growth of private lending.

stories.title: Africa is not a blanket high-risk region

body.text: In this FAZ interview, an expert disputes the blanket characterisation of the African continent as a high-risk region for investment.
The conversation argues for a more differentiated view of investment opportunities across Africa.

shortBody.text: In this FAZ interview, an expert disputes the blanket characterisation of the African continent as a high-risk region for investment.
The conversation argues for a more differentiated view of investment opportunities across Africa.

stories.title: How to build wealth over the long term

body.text: This FAZ commentary sets out principles for long-term wealth creation aimed at German investors in a period of geopolitical uncertainty.
It offers a framework for investing through turbulent times.

shortBody.text: This FAZ commentary sets out principles for long-term wealth creation aimed at German investors in a period of geopolitical uncertainty.
